name: Speed of light
frameworkValue: Structurally determined as universal phase propagation speed through coherence geometry. $L = cT$ from loop closure.
notes: Numerical value reflects human unit conventions. Framework determines $c$ uniquely from axioms + S1 (pseudo-Riemannian structure).

name: Reduced Planck constant
frameworkValue: Minimum coherence cost of one observer cycle on $S^1$. Identified with arc length of minimal closed geodesic via Lyusternik–Fet theorem.
gaps: Absolute numerical value not computed from first principles
notes: Structure (minimum action, quantization, uncertainty principle) all rigorously derived. Numerical value is a unit convention.

name: Newton's gravitational constant
frameworkValue: Constrained: $G = \ell_{\min}^2 c^3/\hbar$ where $\ell_{\min}$ is the minimum resolvable scale. Three convergent arguments (Jacobson thermodynamic, minimal observer self-consistency, curvature-spacetime bridge) establish this relationship. Full derivation requires independent determination of $\ell_{\min}$.
gaps: Independent derivation of $\ell_{\min}$ from axioms (curvature-spacetime bridge conjecture)
notes: Three independent arguments converge on $G = \ell_{\min}^2 c^3/\hbar$. The coupling strength is structurally constrained by the coherence Lagrangian, not fully free. Draft status: the curvature-spacetime bridge conjecture (Conjecture 5.3) would complete the derivation.

name: Fine structure constant
frameworkValue: Determined by the same constraint as $\sin^2\!\theta_W$: once $\Lambda_{\text{EW}}$ is fitted, $\alpha_{\text{em}}^{-1} = \alpha_Y^{-1} + \alpha_2^{-1}$ is fixed. Two-loop: $\alpha_{\text{em}}^{-1}(M_Z) = 128.1$.
gaps: Independent prediction of $\Lambda_{\text{EW}}$ from bootstrap dynamics
notes: Not an independent prediction from $\sin^2\!\theta_W$ — both follow from the same 1-parameter fit ($\Lambda_{\text{EW}}$). The 0.1% accuracy reflects two-loop RG precision, not a separate test.

name: Strong coupling constant
frameworkValue: Suggestive: $1/\dim(\mathbb{O}) = 1/8 = 0.125 \approx \alpha_s(M_Z)$ ($\sim 6\%$ agreement). Requires $\text{SU}(3)$ crystallization near $M_Z$, which is not independently derived. The ratio $\alpha_3/\alpha_2 = 2$ at a common EW scale fails (Landau pole).
notes: The near-coincidence $1/8 \approx 0.118$ is intriguing but cannot be promoted to a prediction without an independent $\Lambda_3$. No gauge unification predicted — falsifiable.

name: Weinberg angle
frameworkValue: $\sin^2\!\theta_W = 1/3$ at algebraic scale (from $\mathbb{C} \subset \mathbb{H}$ embedding). Fitted to experiment by choosing $\Lambda_{\text{EW}}$. Two-loop: $\Lambda_{\text{EW}} = 1.26 \times 10^{10}$ GeV (3.3% shift from 1-loop).
gaps: Independent derivation of $\Lambda_{\text{EW}}$ from bootstrap dynamics
notes: Algebraic boundary condition $\sin^2\!\theta_W = 1/3$ is a genuine structural prediction. The low-energy value is fitted (1 parameter). Two-loop analysis confirms 1-loop is reliable (3.3% shift in $\Lambda_{\text{EW}}$).

name: QCD vacuum angle
frameworkValue: $\theta_{\text{QCD}} = 0$ exactly. Non-associativity of octonions constrains QCD vacuum topology; associator obstruction prevents non-trivial instanton sectors.
gaps: Formal proof that octonionic bundles have trivial instanton moduli space
notes: Predicts no QCD axion — all axion searches (ADMX, CASPEr, IAXO) should return null. Falsifiable.

name: Higgs VEV (electroweak scale)
frameworkValue: Mechanism derived (Coleman-Weinberg coherence crystallization, top-quark loop dominance). Numerical value $v \approx 246$ GeV requires full RG flow of coherence Lagrangian.
gaps: Computing $v$ from coherence Lagrangian RG flow
notes: The instability condition ($6y_t^2 > 9g^2/4 + 3\lambda$) is rigorous. Hierarchy protection via bootstrap dimensional transmutation — no SUSY needed.

name: Higgs boson mass
frameworkValue: $m_h = \sqrt{2\lambda}\,v$. Both $\lambda$ and $v$ follow from the coherence potential shape near crystallization, but neither is computed from first principles.
notes: The formula is exact; the inputs ($\lambda$, $v$) are not independently predicted.

name: Higgs self-coupling
frameworkValue: Depends on shape of coherence potential near crystallization. Not independently predicted.
notes: Requires non-perturbative treatment of the coherence potential at the crystallization transition.

name: Top quark mass
frameworkValue: $m_t = y_t v/\sqrt{2}$ where $y_t \propto \exp(-\alpha_3/g_{\text{EW}}^2)$. Third generation most aligned with EW axis, hence heaviest.
notes: All quark masses share the same mechanism: $y_k \propto \exp(-\alpha_k/g^2)$, with $\alpha_1 > \alpha_2 > \alpha_3$. The exponential hierarchy is rigorous; the specific angles are not.

name: Charm quark mass
frameworkValue: Second generation: intermediate alignment with EW axis. Ratio $m_t/m_c \sim \exp((\alpha_2 - \alpha_3)/g^2) \sim 130$.
notes: See top quark entry for shared mechanism.

name: Up quark mass
frameworkValue: First generation: least aligned with EW axis, hence lightest. Ratio $m_c/m_u \sim 600$ consistent with mechanism.
notes: See top quark entry for shared mechanism.

name: Bottom quark mass
frameworkValue: Third generation down-type. Yukawa from winding geometry overlap with EW axis in down-type sector.
notes: See top quark entry for shared mechanism.

name: Strange quark mass
frameworkValue: Second generation down-type.
notes: See top quark entry for shared mechanism.

name: Down quark mass
frameworkValue: First generation down-type.
notes: See top quark entry for shared mechanism.

name: Tau mass
frameworkValue: Third generation charged lepton. Same winding geometry mechanism as quarks.
notes: See top quark entry for shared mechanism.

name: Muon mass
frameworkValue: Second generation charged lepton.
notes: See top quark entry for shared mechanism.

name: Electron mass
frameworkValue: First generation charged lepton. Lightest because most misaligned from EW axis.
notes: See top quark entry for shared mechanism.

name: Neutrino masses (3)
frameworkValue: Type-I seesaw: $m_\nu = m_D^2/M_R$ with $M_R \sim y_R v$ at EW scale (not GUT). Normal ordering $m_1 < m_2 < m_3$ rigorously derived. Majorana nature from pseudo-real $\text{SU}(2)$. Absolute scale requires $\varepsilon_\nu$ overlap coefficient.
gaps: Absolute mass scale from winding geometry
notes: Falsifiable: predicts Majorana nature (testable via $0\nu\beta\beta$), normal ordering (JUNO/DUNE), and $M_R \sim 100$–$1000$ GeV (colliders).

name: CKM mixing angles (3)
frameworkValue: Small angles explained by steep quark mass hierarchy: $\theta_{ij} \sim V_{ij}/(m_i - m_j)$. $A_5$ symmetry breaking determines structure. Quantitative values require winding geometry.
accuracy: Hierarchy (small angles) derived; values not computed
notes: Near-diagonal CKM structure is a rigorous consequence of the steep mass hierarchy.

name: CKM CP-violating phase
frameworkValue: Nonzero phase geometrically unavoidable with 3 generations (Kobayashi-Maskawa). Discrete value from $A_5$ breaking channel. Existence rigorous; specific value requires channel selection.
accuracy: Existence derived; value from discrete set
notes: CP violation requires $\geq 3$ generations — this is a theorem, not a postulate.

name: PMNS mixing angles (3)
frameworkValue: Large angles from mild neutrino mass hierarchy. Golden ratio prediction: $\tan\theta_{12} = 1/\varphi \to \theta_{12} \approx 31.7°$. $\theta_{23} \approx \pi/4 \approx 45°$. $\theta_{13}$ small (controlled by $A_5$ breaking parameter).
gaps: RG running from symmetry scale to measurement scale
notes: Golden ratio prediction ($\tan\theta_{12} = 1/\varphi$) is the most precise semi-quantitative result. Falsifiable by precision PMNS measurements.

name: PMNS CP-violating phase
frameworkValue: One of five discrete values from $A_5$ breaking channels. Current data favor $\delta \sim -\pi/2$ (maximal CP violation), consistent with one channel.
accuracy: Discrete prediction (5 values); channel selection open
notes: Directly testable by late 2020s. Five discrete values is a strong structural prediction vs. continuous SM parameter.

name: Cosmological constant
frameworkValue: Sign: $\Lambda \geq 0$ (rigorous, from coherence conservation). Upper bound: $\Lambda < 3/\ell_P^2$ (rigorous). Observed tiny value: 120 orders below Planck bound — qualitatively explained but not quantitatively derived.
accuracy: Sign and bound correct; value not derived
notes: The observed $10^{-122}$ hierarchy is the framework's main quantitative failure, shared with all known approaches.

name: Electroweak crystallization direction
smValue: (not a SM parameter — implicit in Yukawa couplings)
frameworkValue: A point on $S^2$ specifying the Higgs crystallization direction relative to the three generation winding axes. Two free real parameters satisfying $\cos^2\!\alpha_1 + \cos^2\!\alpha_2 + \cos^2\!\alpha_3 = 1$.
gaps: Deriving $\hat{n}_{\text{EW}}$ from bootstrap dynamics or crystallization energetics
notes: The crystallization direction determines the intra-type mass hierarchy: the three masses within each fermion type (e.g., $u, c, t$) follow from $y_k \propto \exp(-\alpha_k/g_{\text{eff}}^2)$ with the same three angles $\alpha_k$ but a type-specific effective coupling $g_{\text{eff}}$. The inter-type mass splittings (why $m_t \neq m_b \neq m_\tau$ within each generation) require the CKM and PMNS rotations, which relate the distinct winding-axis triples for up-type, down-type, and lepton sectors (Proposition 5.2 of Three Generations). Numerical testing confirms that the three fermion types have incompatible geometric ratios $R = \ln(m_3/m_2)/\ln(m_2/m_1)$: $R_{\text{up}} \approx 0.91$, $R_{\text{down}} \approx 1.31$, $R_{\text{lepton}} \approx 0.53$. A shared crystallization direction with type-specific couplings fits each mass to within a factor of ~1.4 (RMS log error 0.33). The original claim of '2 parameters replacing 9 Yukawa couplings' is overstated; the actual parameter content is $\hat{n}_{\text{EW}}$ (2 DOF) + CKM/PMNS rotations (7–9 DOF) + effective coupling ratios.

name: W boson mass
frameworkValue: $m_W = gv/2$ — follows exactly from electroweak breaking once $v$ and $g$ are known.
notes: Not an independent parameter — determined by $v$ and gauge couplings.

name: Z boson mass
frameworkValue: $m_Z = m_W/\cos\theta_W$ — follows exactly from electroweak breaking.
notes: Not an independent parameter — determined by $v$ and gauge couplings.
